polygon.data-contents: The corsairs' open city -- every flag welcome, every purse at risk.
Storm-shrine and last dry harbor of the sinking Tidereach.
First city of the League; a forest of masts and a thicket of knives.
Where the world's eldest trees stand; the fey hold their Greenmoot here.
Cathedral-fortress of the Iron Covenant; the Eternal Pyre never gutters.
Seat of King Aldric IV of Aurenne; a glorious, crumbling palace-city.
The great inland lake; pilgrim-barges and a sunken pre-Sundering villa.
Free-company clearinghouse: contracts signed, companies hired, scores settled.
The only walled town of the Sunscorch; ivory, fever, and beast-god drums.
The dwarven hold-city; its Iron Gate has not opened to outsiders in 80 years.
Greatest of the Frostmark steadings; a free moot-hall on the only sheltered fjord.
The drowned imperial capital at the Scar's heart. The richest, deadliest ruin alive.
First of the Pallid Cities; beautiful, poisoned, ruled by the masked Ash-Duke.
Greatest of the brass-domed caliphate cities; caravans, courts, and conspiracies.
A still-standing imperial arch that leaks wild magic and worse.
A black gate the dwarves walled up before the Sundering. Something knocks.
The bridge-city where the Silverflow trade meets the eastern roads.
Banking heart of the continent; its Senate Vaults are said to be undelvable.
The widest sinkhole-gate to the Underrealm; a downward road no map follows.
A black step-pyramid where the Serpent's oldest rite is still kept.
The serpent-throne; its god-king has 'reigned' through nine recorded deaths.
The strongest Boneland warcamp this year; it will have a new owner next year.
A khan's tomb whose dead are said to sing the names of those who enter.
The moving capital of the Khalassar -- wherever the Great Khan's yurt stands.
The Evenstar Court of Yselmark, a silver city woven through living trees.
A pre-Sundering elven tower now half-sunk in a black mere; its library survives.

svg.text: The continent of The Known World, two centuries after the Sundering. Twenty realms; one healing wound at the heart.
